weather_report:
Subiaco wakes under a lucid, lacquer-blue sky, the sun already sharpening edges and coaxing a soft glare from shopfront glass. The air feels clean and dry, with long views and little in the way of haze. Through late morning into midafternoon the day looks settled and sunny, the sort that makes colors ring true. Expect the UV to climb quickly toward midday; the light will be generous, and unshielded skin will notice it.

Dress for warmth that builds: light, breathable fabrics, a hat that earns its keep, sunglasses, and a touch of sunscreen. It’s a good day to move at an easy pace—walk your errands along Rokeby Road before lunch, let the washing take the sun, tend a few pots on the balcony, or sit in the dappled shade with a book and a cool drink. If you’re heading out later, favor shade and water over bravado, then ride the afternoon sea breeze home when it stirs.
